message:
porting(br-2bmq): move coverage budget runner orchestration into Rust

What changed
- Added `live_oracle_budget_orchestrator` in `fr-conformance`.
- Moved runner selection (`FR_BUDGET_RUNNER`) and command construction out of
  shell and into typed/tested Rust orchestration.
- Converted `scripts/check_coverage_flake_budget.sh` into a thin delegator to
  the new Rust orchestrator.

Behavior preserved
- Still enforces exactly one positional `<coverage_summary.json>` argument.
- Supports local and remote execution backends via `--runner` and
  `FR_BUDGET_RUNNER` (`local|rch`).
- Continues to execute `live_oracle_budget_gate` with deterministic `cmd=`
  output for traceability.

Why
- Eliminates remaining shell control flow for coverage budget execution.
- Keeps script UX stable while centralizing orchestration in Rust for
  consistency with the ongoing porting track.

Validation
- `cargo fmt --all` and `cargo fmt --check`
- `~/.local/bin/rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets`
- `~/.local/bin/rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `~/.local/bin/rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-conformance -- --nocapture`
- `ubs --only=rust crates/fr-conformance/src/bin/live_oracle_budget_orchestrator.rs`

files.patch:
@@ -8,8 +8,8 @@ Usage:
   FR_BUDGET_RUNNER=rch ./scripts/check_coverage_flake_budget.sh <coverage_summary.json>
 
 Description:
-  Thin wrapper around the Rust implementation:
-  cargo run -p fr-conformance --bin live_oracle_budget_gate -- <coverage_summary.json>
+  Thin wrapper around the Rust orchestrator:
+  cargo run -p fr-conformance --bin live_oracle_budget_orchestrator -- <coverage_summary.json>
 
 Runner knobs (env):
   FR_BUDGET_RUNNER         local (default) or rch
@@ -22,15 +22,7 @@ if [[ "${1:-}" == "-h" || "${1:-}" == "--help" || $# -ne 1 ]]; then
   exit 2
 fi
 
-RUNNER="${FR_BUDGET_RUNNER:-local}"
-cmd=(
-  cargo run -p fr-conformance --bin live_oracle_budget_gate --
-  "$1"
-)
-
-if [[ "$RUNNER" == "rch" ]]; then
-  cmd=(~/.local/bin/rch exec -- "${cmd[@]}")
-fi
+cmd=(cargo run -p fr-conformance --bin live_oracle_budget_orchestrator -- "$1")
 
 printf 'cmd='
 printf '%q ' "${cmd[@]}"
